_comment: Hardware calibration for Ascend 910B/910B3 — derived from chunk_kda profiling vs DES trace
_derived_from: chunk_kda_bwd_kernel (MIX_AIC, 4096 blocks), verified against split_qkv (AIV-only, 20 blocks)

_findings.e2e_formula: E2E = max(waves_AIC * per_block_AIC, waves_AIV * per_block_AIV) + grid_blocks * launch_overhead
_findings._verified: chunk_kda model gives 111,384us + 508us overhead = 111,892us (exact match with real)
_findings.scalar_bottleneck: AIV scalar ALU is 1-wide, serializing all PIPE_S ops. This creates an unavoidable bottleneck.
_findings.scalar_aiv_share: 84% of AIV wall time is scalar. DES correctly models this, but each scalar op takes ~4x more real cycles than DES assumes.
_findings.cross_kernel_note: E2E model/real ratio varies (0.50 vs 0.14 for chunk vs split) because kernel structure determines which pipe bottlenecks. This is CORRECT behavior — DES should NOT produce a constant E2E ratio.

per_pipe_cycle_multipliers._description: Multiply DES cycle count (1 per op) by these to get real hardware cycles
per_pipe_cycle_multipliers._usage: DES op duration (cycles) × multiplier = real hardware cycles for that pipe

aic_mac.note: AIC Cube MAC — pipeline depth ~21, well-utilized

aic_scalar.note: AIC Scalar ALU — single-issue, 28x slower than DES 1-cycle model

aiv_vec.note: AIV Vector ALU — SIMD pipelined, ~2.4x real cycles per DES cycle

aiv_mte2_v.note: AIV MTE2 (L1→L0) — close to vector rate

aiv_scalar.note: AIV Scalar ALU — single-issue, 3.9x slower. DOMINATES AIV wall time (84%).

scalar_serialization._description: Scalar ALU is 1-wide. When multiple scalar ops queue up, they serialize.
scalar_serialization.max_issue_rate: 1 scalar op per cycle per core — cannot be parallelized
scalar_serialization._impact: For kernels with >10% scalar ops, scalar ALU becomes the E2E bottleneck. This is a HARDWARE LIMIT, not a modeling error.

memory_overhead_us_per_transaction._description: Fixed latency overhead per MTE transaction, independent of data size
